steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim is that Winnemucca creates suspense by emphasizing her physical response to an event. The correct quote must show (1) a tense or threatening event and (2) a bodily reaction — racing heart, trembling, breathlessness — that builds tension.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "Oh, how my heart jumped when I heard a noise close by. It was a horse running towards us. We had to lie down close to the ground. It came close to us and stopped. Oh, how my heart beat! I thought whoever it was would hear my heart beat.": A horse approaches in the dark; Winnemucca's pounding heart is described twice, directly conveying physical fear and building suspense. ✓
  • Choice B — "Oh, how happy everybody was! One could hear laughter everywhere, and songs were sung by happy women and children.": This is joyful celebration, not suspense, and describes no physical distress. ✗
  • Choice C — "Late in that fall, there came news that my grandfather was on his way home. Then my father took a great many of his men and went to meet his father, and there came back a runner, saying, that all our people must come together.": A factual account of events with no physical response and no tension. ✗
  • Choice D — "That same fall, after my grandfather came home, he told my father to take charge of his people and hold the tribe, as he was going back to California with as many of his people as he could get to go with him.": Narrates a directive with no physical reaction or suspense. ✗
A is correct.
